title: AI conversation rows outlive deleted users
reasoning: The migration stores AI conversation content but leaves both user_id columns unconstrained and leaves message conversation_id as an index only. In this app the assistant is user-scoped and account deletion hard-deletes the user, so private financial chat content can remain orphaned with stale user IDs. The missing conversation foreign key also means database-level integrity does not protect messages from becoming orphaned outside the controller's manual delete path.
reproduction: Create a user with an AI conversation and message, then delete the user account. The user row is gone, but the agent_conversations and agent_conversation_messages rows remain because no foreign key or account-deletion cleanup applies.
recommendation: Decide the retention policy explicitly. For privacy, add a forward migration that cascades or nulls user references and constrains agent_conversation_messages.conversation_id to agent_conversations.id with cascadeOnDelete; alternatively add account-deletion cleanup/anonymization and keep the schema intentionally unconstrained with tests.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: AiChatTest covers deleting a conversation through the AI endpoint, and ProfileUpdateTest covers deleting the user, but no test combines account deletion with existing AI conversation rows.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a feature test that seeds an AI conversation/message for a user, deletes the account through profile.destroy, and asserts the configured retention behavior: rows are deleted, anonymized, or user_id is nulled.
minimumFixScope: Forward migration for AI conversation referential integrity or explicit account-deletion cleanup plus a profile deletion regression test.
